system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

vertices

    (0    0    0.001)
    (0.02 0    0.001)
    (0.02 0.02 0.001)
    (0    0.02 0.001)
);

blocks
(

);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    fuel
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    air
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {

        faces

            (0 3 2 1)

        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='chemistryProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

chemistry       on;

initialChemicalTimeStep 1e-07;

odeCoeffs

    absTol          1e-12;
    relTol          1e-01;
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.reactingFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword species is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_20260516_195157_run1/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/1/constant/physicalProperties"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_20260516_195157_run1/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/1/constant/physicalProperties from line 12 to line 27.

    From function const Foam::entry& Foam::dictionary::lookupEntry(const Foam::word&, bool, bool) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 831.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is:

```
keyword species is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means that in **OpenFOAM 10**, the `physicalProperties` file needs a `species` sub-dictionary (or entry) because the `multiComponentMixture` model requires it to know what species are present and their properties.

Looking at the current `physicalProperties` file, it contains the thermo-type configuration and references to the chemistry reader/files, but it lacks the `species` keyword/sub-dictionary that `multiComponentMixture` expects.

## Root Cause

In OpenFOAM 10 with `multiComponentMixture`, the `physicalProperties` dictionary must include a `species` entry listing all species and their individual thermo data paths. Without this, the solver cannot determine which species are being modeled.

## Proposed Solution

Add a `species` sub-dictionary inside `constant/physicalProperties` that lists each species and points to its thermodynamic data. Based on the reference case structure and the species defined in the `reactions` file (CH4, O2, N2, H2O, CO2), the `species` section should look something like:

```
species
(
    CH4
    {
        type        specie;
        thermoDataFile "thermo.compressibleGas";
    }
    O2
    {
        type        specie;
        thermoDataFile "thermo.compressibleGas";
    }
    N2
    {
        type        specie;
        thermoDataFile "thermo.compressibleGas";
    }
    H2O
    {
        type        specie;
        thermoDataFile "thermo.compressibleGas";
    }
    CO2
    {
        type        specie;
        thermoDataFile "thermo.compressibleGas";
    }
);
```

Additionally, verify that the `thermo.compressibleGas` file contains proper species-specific thermodynamic data for all five species (CH4, O2, N2, H2O, CO2). The current `thermo.compressibleGas` file appears to only contain generic/global thermo settings without per-species entries. It may need to include individual species blocks within the `specie` sub-dictionary pointing to their respective thermo data files.

If the `thermo.compressibleGas` file does not contain per-species thermo data, another approach would be to add inline species thermo data directly into `physicalProperties` under the `species` sub-dictionary, specifying `A`, `B`, `C`, `D` coefficients and temperature ranges for each species (similar to how the `thermo.compressibleGas` tutorial handles it).

## Summary

1. **Primary fix**: Add a `species` sub-dictionary to `constant/physicalProperties` listing CH4, O2, N2, H2O, CO2 with their thermo data file references.
2. **Secondary check**: Ensure `thermo.compressibleGas` contains valid per-species thermodynamic data blocks referenced by those species entries.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'constant/physicalProperties', 'changes': 'Add a species sub-dictionary inside the main dictionary containing entries for CH4, O2, N2, H2O, CO2 each with type specie and thermoDataFile referencing thermo.compressibleGas'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
